title: Code index prefers SCIP with LSP fallback
summary: Kage code index should prefer the external SCIP path for JS/TS repos when scip typescript and the scip CLI are installed, convert the generated index into .agent memory/code index/scip.json, and fall back to the built in
body: Kage code-index should prefer the external SCIP path for JS/TS repos when scip-typescript and the scip CLI are installed, convert the generated index into .agent_memory/code_index/scip.json, and fall back to the built-in LSP-compatible symbol index when external tools are unavailable. This keeps first-run setup light while giving large repos a real code-intelligence path. Verified by: npm test --prefix mcp

context.fact: Kage code-index should prefer the external SCIP path for JS/TS repos when scip-typescript and the scip CLI are installed, convert the generated index into .agent_memory/code_index/scip.json, and fall back to the built-in LSP-compatible symbol index when external tools are unavailable. This keeps first-run setup light while giving large repos a real code-intelligence path. Verified by: npm test --prefix mcp
